nodes.context: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in California, involved in organized crime and violent conflict with other clubs.
Motorcycle club from which Otto Friedli broke to start the Hells Angels.
Motorcycle club; Dick White, a member, is said to have founded the Hells Angels in 1951.
Motorcycle club; former members reportedly founded the San Francisco Hells Angels charter in 1954.
Biker club; former members James and Pat Miles founded the Sacramento Hells Angels charter.
International outlaw motorcycle club founded in California, involved in organized crime and violence.
Black motorcycle club formed by Tobie Levingston, has a mutual friendship with Hells Angels.
African-American motorcycle club that partied with Hells Angels and was offered patches in the 1970s.
Motorcycle club in Vancouver that merged with Hells Angels in 1983.
Montreal-based motorcycle club; member Gregory Woolley was associate of Hells Angels Montreal charter.
Motorcycle club that patched over to form Hells Angels' Montreal charter in 1977.
Group that led the 'Alliance to fight the Angels' during the Quebec Biker War.
Official and most prominent support club of the Hells Angels, with chapters in nearly 20 countries.
Support group in Denmark consisting of young males who do not own motorcycles.
Support group in Sweden consisting of young males who do not own motorcycles.
One of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs in the U.S., alongside Hells Angels.
One of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs in the U.S., alongside Hells Angels.
One of the 'big four' motorcycle gangs in the U.S. and part of the 'big six' in Australia.
Part of the 'big six' motorcycle gangs in Australia.
Part of the 'big six' motorcycle gangs in Australia.
Part of the 'big six' motorcycle gangs in Australia.
Part of the 'big six' motorcycle gangs in Australia.

edges.evidence_quote: Otto Friedli, a World War II veteran, is credited with starting the club after breaking from the Pissed Off Bastards motorcycle club over a feud with a rival gang.
edges.context: Hells Angels founder broke from Pissed Off Bastards, indicating a split.

edges.evidence_quote: According to an alternative theory, the Hells Angels were founded on November 15, 1951, in San Bernardino, by Dick White, a member of the Redlands Road Runners.
edges.context: Alternative founding theory ties Hells Angels to a member of Redlands Road Runners.

edges.evidence_quote: The San Francisco ("Frisco") charter was reportedly founded by former members of the Market Street Commandos in 1954.
edges.context: San Francisco Hells Angels charter founded by former Market Street Commandos members.

edges.evidence_quote: A North Sacramento charter was established in 1956, followed by another charter in Sacramento the following year, which was formed by two brothers, James "Mother" Miles and Pat Miles, who were former members of the Hell Bent for Glory biker club.
edges.context: Sacramento Hells Angels charter founded by former Hell Bent for Glory members.

edges.evidence_quote: Tobie Levingston, who formed the black motorcycle club East Bay Dragons MC, wrote in his book that he and Barger have a long-lasting friendship and that the Hells Angels and Dragons have a mutual friendship and hang out and ride together.
edges.context: Hells Angels and East Bay Dragons have a mutual friendship and socialize together.

edges.evidence_quote: At one point in the 1970s, the Hells Angels sought to consolidate the various motorcycle clubs and offered every member of the Chosen Few a Hells Angels patch, an offer that was declined.
edges.context: Hells Angels offered Chosen Few members patches to join them in the 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Satan's Angels MC in Vancouver had a black member when it merged with the Hells Angels in 1983.
edges.context: Satan's Angels MC merged with Hells Angels in 1983.

edges.evidence_quote: Woolley became an associate of the Hells Angels Montreal charter in the 1990s
edges.context: Rockers MC member Gregory Woolley became an associate of Hells Angels Montreal charter.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1977, the Hells Angels arrived in Canada with the Popeye Moto Club patching over to form the Hells Angels' Montreal charter.
edges.context: Popeye Moto Club patched over to become Hells Angels Montreal charter in 1977.

edges.evidence_quote: The war began when the Hells Angels in Quebec began to try to establish a monopoly on street-level drug sales in Quebec. Many drug dealers and crime families resisted and established groups such as the 'Alliance to fight the Angels', led by the Rock Machine.
edges.context: Rock Machine led the 'Alliance to fight the Angels' against Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War.

edges.evidence_quote: The Red Devils Motorcycle Club, a biker group with chapters in nearly 20 countries, is the official and most prominent support club of the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Red Devils is the official support club of Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels have also formed support groups, such as AK81 in Denmark
edges.context: AK81 is a support group formed by Hells Angels in Denmark.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels have also formed support groups, such as AK81 in Denmark and the Red & White Crew in Sweden
edges.context: Red & White Crew is a support group formed by Hells Angels in Sweden.
